patent_context.patent_title: Smart garden irrigation system with adaptive scheduling
patent_context.claim_description: A smart irrigation controller comprising a soil moisture sensor, a wireless communication module for transmitting sensor data, and an adaptive watering schedule algorithm configured to adjust irrigation timing based on real-time soil moisture readings

claims.claim_text: A smart irrigation controller comprising a soil moisture sensor, a wireless communication module for transmitting sensor data, and an adaptive watering schedule algorithm configured to adjust irrigation timing based on real-time soil moisture readings

structured_elements.text: wireless communication module for transmitting sensor data
adaptive watering schedule algorithm configured to adjust irrigation timing
based on real-time soil moisture readings

claims.accused_product_description: The GreenBot G5 controller includes a capacitive soil sensor, Bluetooth data link to a phone app, and a preset watering timer that users can manually adjust after checking soil readings on their phone.

element_mapping_facts.e2: Bluetooth data link may or may not constitute a 'wireless communication module' — the claim may require a dedicated module rather than a phone link

claims.disputed_element_notes: The term 'wireless communication module' is disputed — does a Bluetooth phone link qualify as a 'module'? The phrase 'adaptive watering schedule algorithm' is also contested — the accused product uses manual timer adjustment, not an autonomous algorithm. The term 'configured to' may imply either inherent capability or active configuration.
claims.specification_notes: The patent specification describes a dedicated wireless radio module and an on-device algorithm that operates without user intervention. This suggests a narrower interpretation of both the communication module and the adaptive algorithm.
